title: EBA Guidelines on ICT and Security Risk Management
summary: EBA guidelines establishing requirements for ICT and security risk management in financial institutions. Covers governance, ICT risk management framework, ICT operations, ICT project management, and information security.

key_requirements.title: ICT governance and strategy
key_requirements.description: Management body must approve and oversee ICT strategy and risk management framework

key_requirements.title: ICT risk management framework
key_requirements.description: Comprehensive framework covering identification, assessment, mitigation, and monitoring of ICT risks

key_requirements.title: Information security
key_requirements.description: Implement security controls for confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data and systems

key_requirements.title: ICT change management
key_requirements.description: Formal change management process with testing, approval, and rollback procedures

key_requirements.title: ICT incident management
key_requirements.description: Process for detecting, logging, analyzing, and responding to ICT incidents

engineering_implications.description: Implement access controls, encryption, and security monitoring
Establish comprehensive testing procedures for all changes
Log all security events, access attempts, and system changes
Design secure architecture with defense-in-depth principles

source_references.citation: EBA/GL/2019/04 - Guidelines on ICT and security risk management
